sessions.intent: Translate architect's coverage matrix into failing-test contract: C# TUnit stubs, PLang .goal stubs, and 4 integration cuts.

before.assumptions: Architect handed off a complete coverage matrix (~60 rows) + failure matrix (~19 rows) + new-surfaces inventory across 5 stages. Test-strategy fixes 4 integration cuts. Test-designer owns the final test surface — matrix is suggestion, not gospel (Ingi reconfirmed up front).
before.risk: Stage 1 + Stage 2 are tightly coupled. If coder splits them into separate PRs, the Stage 1 tests will reference renamed surfaces (Type/Extension) that don't compile until Stage 2 is also merged. Mitigation: stubs are Assert.Fail anyway — they only need the architecture to settle, not the build to be green at every intermediate commit.

actions.context: Stage 1 — ISerializer input tightened to Data, OBP renames on serializer/channel families. Reflection-based assertions for the 'old name gone' rows (1.2, ContentType property removed, GetByContentType method removed, etc.) — they can't be TUnit bodies, the compile-time guard is the real assertion.

Stage 2 — merged application/plang serializer, sign-if-missing in wire converter, canonicalization fix (crypto.Hash via Transport.ForOutbound), Json composition extensions. Added Json.ForInbound symmetry test (new-surfaces inventory mentions it but no matrix row pins it) and a byte[]-leaf wire-converter test (Stage 3's flat Compress relies on it).
Stage 3 — flat Compress. Nine C# tests pin the shape change and the deletion of _envelopeJsonOptions + RehydrateNestedData; the .goal pins the PLang developer-facing round-trip.

Stage 4 — Properties get a wire scope. ~20 C# tests pin the nested wire object, primitive round-trips (incl. JSON int→long promotion), unconstrained keys, the unknown-top-level-field-is-ignored rule, and canonicalization binding Properties. Five .goal tests pin the developer-facing %x!key% navigation including bang vs negation-prefix disambiguation.

Integration cuts 1–3. Architect's strategy doc fixed these as the end-to-end contract; I translated each into ~4 TUnit methods covering wire-shape assertions, value/signature preservation, and the tamper-fails-verify guarantee per cut.

Stage 5 vocabulary sweep (kept rows 5.1 + 5.3; pruned 5.2/5.4/5.5/5.6 as CI invariants, not unit tests) and consolidated failure-matrix rows (EnsureSigned without Context, Properties unsupported value type, Decompress malformed input, channel direction violations, crypto unsupported algorithm).
Plan, verdict, and rolling summary.

after.status: Contract written. 11 C# files + 4 integration-cut classes + 7 .goal files. ~90 test methods + 7 goal stubs, all failing (Assert.Fail / throw not implemented). `dotnet build PLang.Tests` clean — 0 errors.
after.health: Ready for coder. Coder should work in stage order (1→2→3→4→5); stages 1 and 2 likely merge as one PR per architect's note.
after.notes: Pruned non-unit-test rows from matrix (5.2 git grep, 5.4 var renames, 5.5/5.6 build-clean tautologies). Added two tests not in matrix: WireConverter byte[]-leaf emission (Stage 3 dependency) and Json.ForInbound symmetry (new-surfaces inventory orphan). Regrouped files by topic (PropertiesWireShapeTests, WireConverterSigningTests) rather than by stage number — easier to maintain when stages converge in PRs.

sessions.intent: Design Data's variable→wire→variable round trip: flat shape, signing at channel, merge the two plang serializers, drop Envelope.

before.assumptions: Two plang serializers (application/plang and application/plang+data) duplicate STJ setup. The plang+data serializer builds a parallel Envelope class to bypass [JsonIgnore] on Signature. Stream.WriteCore strips the Data wrapper before handing to the serializer. Compress double-wraps (gzip Data inside archived Data). ISerializer accepts object?, but PLang only ever ships Data.
before.risk: Touches the channel write path and four serializer implementations atomically. Any caller that relied on the polymorphic object? input or on the data.Value strip will break — but per Ingi's assertion, those callers don't exist in PLang's actual flow because Data is the universal currency.

actions.context: Spine + wire-shape reference. Spine carries the design narrative and stage index; wire-shape carries concrete JSON examples for plain / compressed / encrypted / nested-value cases.

Four stage files. 1 tightens ISerializer to Data + unstrips Stream.WriteCore. 2 merges the plang serializers, drops Envelope, moves EnsureSigned to Channel. 3 flattens Compress. 4 vocabulary sweep — drop 'envelope' word.
Chronological summary log; initial entry.

Added 'Data Is Opaque to Its Consumers' principle under Core Philosophy. Captures the insight from this session: every layer in the pipeline handles Data as an opaque unit, only the encoder at the leaf walks properties.

after.status: Initial design plan complete. Four stages carved, wire-shape reference written, summary logged. Ready for test-designer to produce test-strategy.md and test-coverage.md, or for coder to begin Stage 1.
after.health: Design is settled. Conversation with Ingi covered the round-trip semantics, name as addressability coordinate, signing position, and the flat wire shape. No open architectural questions.
after.notes: Out of scope and explicitly parked: algorithm tagging on archived/encryption (the +variant pattern), protobuf encoder, Encrypt path flattening (waits for crypto service), inspection-path serialization (data.ToString for debug).

sessions.intent: Merge runtime2 into data-serialize-cleanup; reflect typed-action-returns in plan + Stage 1

before.assumptions: Plan and stages were carved against the pre-merge serializer surface. ISerializer returned bare T? and threw on parse errors. Stage 1 was going to tighten both return shape and input shape in one stroke.
before.risk: The merge could have changed the surface enough to invalidate other stages — needs an audit before claiming the plan still stands.

actions.context: Merged origin/runtime2 cleanly. Confirmed: Stages 2/3/4 unchanged; Stage 1 splits — return half done by typed-action-returns commit 5b1b894c4, input half remains.

plan.md problem #3 + ISerializer decision section + stage index annotated. Stage 1 rewritten to enumerate done-vs-remaining and narrow scope to input-tightening. Stage 2 code example updated to Task<Data> + try/catch. Summary gets a new top entry recording the merge and the changes.

after.status: Plan is reconciled with runtime2 surface. Stage 1 is narrower but still required (input side); other stages stand.
after.notes: Coder/test-designer pickup should read stage-1-iserializer-data.md fresh — the done-list at the top prevents re-doing the merged work.

sessions.intent: Lock in design pivots from review: sign-in-converter, canonicalization fix, Properties-on-wire (new Stage 4); rewrite plan + stages + wire-shape accordingly

before.assumptions: Plan said signing happens at the channel (outermost only); Properties were [JsonIgnore]; the type field in wire-shape used variable-name instead of schema-name; Stage 2 deliverable was "channel signs before WriteCore".
before.risk: The pivot to sign-in-converter touches Stage 2 and Stage 3 narratives at once. Properties-on-wire adds a real architectural stage (C# shape change + wire emission + variable-syntax change). Risk is leaving the stages inconsistent if any one of them keeps the old framing.

actions.context: Rewrote the Signing decision section (sign-if-missing during converter walk, three properties: forwarding, compress, list-of-Data). Added Properties cross-cutting decision (Dictionary<string,object?> primitives, flat top-level wire, %x.field% vs %x!key%, reserved keys, sign-walk-skips). Updated stages table to 5 stages. Updated test-surface bullets to match new behaviour.

Substantial rewrite. Dropped "channel signs before WriteCore" deliverable; replaced with sign-in-converter (wire converter calls EnsureSigned during walk). Added crypto/Default.cs canonicalization fix (hash through wire-direction options). Updated code example, design narrative, risks, and coder verification list.

Replaced the "no signing inside Compress" section (which was wrong) with the new framing: Compress routes through the registered serializer; the converter signs D1 during the byte-conversion automatically; this also fixes today's bug where _envelopeJsonOptions strips Signature from compressed bytes. Updated Properties round-trip note (will survive after Stage 4). Added sign-then-compress verification.

New Stage 4: Properties flatten to the wire. Scope, deliverables (Properties.cs C# rewrite, wire converter Write/Read, %!key% syntax, reserved-key enforcement), design rationale, risks, coder verification. Stage 5 is the renamed vocabulary sweep (was Stage 4).
Renamed to stage-5-vocabulary-sweep.md to make room for Stage 4 = Properties on wire. Vocabulary sweep is unchanged otherwise.

Fixed plain-Data example to show type:"object" for untyped (vs type:"user" for Data<User> in sibling example). Added LLM-response example showing Properties flattened to top-level (cost, model). Updated compressed-Data narrative to show inner Data carries its own signature (sign-during-byte-conversion). Updated nested-Data section to show inner Datas each have signatures, with canonicalization binding the chain. Updated "What's NOT on the wire" and round-trip invariants.
Added new top entry recording the three pivots (signing, canonicalization, Properties) and the new Stage 4. Stage status table updated to 5 stages.

after.status: Plan + 5 stages + wire-shape now consistent with all design decisions from the review session. Stage 1 is partial-complete (return half merged); Stages 2-5 are pending.
after.notes: Ready for coder pickup. Stage 2 carries the biggest surface (merge plang serializers + sign-in-converter + canonicalization). Stage 4 (Properties) is independent enough that it could run in parallel with Stage 3 once Stage 2 lands.

sessions.intent: Properties shape pivot from flat to nested; drop reserved-key rule. Apply edits across plan, Stage 4, and wire-shape.

before.assumptions: Stage 4 emitted Properties as top-level wire fields (flat) and enforced reserved-key validation. Plan Properties section + wire-shape examples + Stage 4 deliverables all reflected the flat shape.
before.risk: Three files describe the same Property shape from different angles. If any one of them keeps the flat shape, the docs drift.

actions.context: Rewrote Properties cross-cutting decision for nested shape. Dropped reserved-key rule. Updated receiver-side rule to "unknown top-level fields ignored". Updated test-surface bullet for Properties round-trip.

Full rewrite. Title "flatten to the wire" → "get a wire scope". Properties C# type drops reserved-key guard. Wire converter Write emits nested properties object (omitted when empty); Read parses 5 reserved fields and silently skips unknown top-level. Design re-argues for nested via four-lens analysis. Coder-verification checks updated to assert nested shape, no reserved-key throw, omit-when-empty, etc.
LLM-response example now shows nested properties object. "What is NOT on the wire" updated for five-field contract + unconstrained Property keys + ignore-unknown rule. Round-trip invariants updated.
New top entry recording the Stage 1 OBP renames and the Properties flat-to-nested pivot. Stage status table updated.

after.status: Plan, Stage 4, wire-shape, and summary now all describe the nested-properties shape consistently. Ready for coder pickup.
after.notes: No reserved-key validation in the Properties C# type — keys are unconstrained because they live in their own object on the wire.

sessions.intent: Write the test-strategy and test-coverage files referenced by plan.md so the test-designer handoff has all the design content it needs.

before.assumptions: plan.md referenced plan/test-strategy.md and plan/test-coverage.md, but the files did not exist on disk. Ingi caught the dangling link before sending to test-designer.
before.risk: Test-designer would have either followed broken links (wasted cycle), derived coverage from scratch (slow, error-prone), or asked architect to write them (cycle delay). Architect character file is explicit these are architect-produced content.

actions.context: Narrative file. Scope statement; test layer mapping (C# / goal / integration); four integration cuts narrated in full (Plain round-trip, Sign-then-compress, Multi-actor forwarding, Properties wire shape); explicit list of what is NOT covered by the cuts.

Reference file. Coverage matrix per stage (~60 rows total): behavior + layer + sense. Consolidated failure matrix (~18 negative paths). New-surfaces inventory: interfaces/types, new methods, renamed/removed surfaces, variable-syntax, existing surfaces touched by reference.
Test surface section reworded — describes what is IN each file rather than "to be written."
New top entry documenting the test-strategy/test-coverage write and the test-designer-handoff readiness.

after.status: plan + 5 stages + wire-shape + test-strategy + test-coverage are all consistent and on disk. Branch is ready for test-designer handoff.
after.notes: Next: run.ps1 test-designer data-serialize-cleanup "Create test suites from the architect plan on branch data-serialize-cleanup" -b data-serialize-cleanup

sessions.intent: Validate test quality on data-serialize-cleanup after codeanalyzer v2 PASS

before.assumptions: test-designer v1 wrote ~90 failing stubs; coder closed Stage 1-5 + F1-F11; codeanalyzer v2 PASS
before.risk: no coder/ folder or baseline-tests.md exists — process violation; cannot distinguish regressions from pre-existing failures without baseline

actions.context: Tester v2 outputs: report, plan, verdict, summary. Two minor weak-assertion findings; process note (no coder/ folder); verdict PASS.

Mutation test: replaced serializer.OutboundOptions with JsonSerializerOptions.Default at line 51 to verify Stage 2 canonicalization coverage. 3 tests correctly failed (CryptoHash_UsesTransportForOutboundOptions_NotDefaultStj, Cut4_TamperingPropertyValue_FailsOuterSignatureVerify, OuterSignature_AfterPropertiesValueTamper_FailsVerify). Mutation reverted; git status clean.

after.health: C# 3229/3229 + PLang 228/228 green on clean rebuild; Stage 2 canonicalization fix mutation-verified; 2 minor weak-assertion findings (cosmetic).
after.notes: Process: no .bot/data-serialize-cleanup/coder/ folder exists — flagged as process violation, not a blocker. Coder did substantive work across Stages 1-5 + F1-F11 but skipped writing tester-handoff artifacts.

sessions.intent: Security review of data-serialize-cleanup: wire converter, canonicalization, sign-if-missing carveouts, Properties round-trips, Sensitive-leak surfaces.

before.assumptions: Codeanalyzer v2 PASSED (11 v1 findings closed). Tester v2 PASSED with mutation verification. Architecture stable. Looking for security-class gaps the structural review and behavioural tests don't catch.
before.risk: Stage 2 widens application/plang's role as inter-actor transport; Sensitive-skip carveout (for settings/sqlite) inherits to every channel using plang mime. Stage 4 Properties on wire introduces a new asymmetric path (write-as-Data, read-as-dict). Stage 1's converter walk has multiple recursion entry points.

actions.context: Read full Write/Read paths, MarkOuterForHash ref-counting (closed v1 F2), LiftDataIfShaped rehydration. Found F1: GetRawText+Deserialize<@this>(string) creates a new Utf8JsonReader with depth=0 on each recursion level — STJ MaxDepth budget restarts, only the C# call stack bounds nesting. Medium DoS.

Hash routes through registered application/plang serializer with MarkOuterForHash carveout. SerializerMismatch 500 on type-mismatch (closed v1 F4). Outer signature transitively binds inner Datas — correct.
Variable.Resolve fails-closed on malformed shapes via IsMalformed; variable.set returns InvalidVariableReference 400. Closed v1 F5.
EnsureSupportedValue is top-level only — IDictionary/IEnumerable accepted structurally. F3 finding: List<Data>/Dict<Data> round-trips asymmetrically. Coder accepted as v1 F9 tradeoff.
100 MB MaxDecompressedSize cap in place. Stage 3 flatten correct. Compress/Decompress route through registered plang serializer with ContextLessFallback single-source-of-truth (closed v1 F10).

Documented intentional Sensitive.Strip omission (for settings/sqlite). F2 finding: any external channel registered with mime=application/plang transports [Sensitive] cleartext. Stage 2 widens this surface.

signing.verify pipeline unchanged from runtime2 base (only vocab edit). NoSignature 400 returned when RawSignature null — receive-side gate holds. Step 7 'if Value != null' skip is unchanged; not introduced by this branch.
semgrep-scan.sh: 14 findings (down from 15 baseline). All serializer-default-options informational hits in pre-existing files. No new violations introduced.
Standard security-pass artefacts.

after.status: PASS — 1 Medium open, 3 Low/Info open. No critical/high.
after.health: Branch merge-ready under the critical/high-blocks-merge rule. F1 (Medium DoS) recommended to land before merge — 5-line fix.
after.notes: F1 is the only newly-introduced finding; F2/F3/F4 are widened-scope or accepted-tradeoff items on known patterns. F1 ratchets to High the moment any external-bytes channel registers with mime=application/plang.
